records.summary: With an explicit --config pointing at a nonexistent file, the reference CLI rejects (no silent discovery fallback).
The reference CLI's compose teardown behavior versus deacon's marker cleanup on --remove-existing-container is not yet characterized by an executable case.
The reference CLI derives the compose project name as <folder>_devcontainer verbatim, which can be invalid (e.g. leading separators) or collide with deacon's project.
The reference CLI accepts an object with a duplicate top-level key and applies last-wins.
The reference CLI v0.87.0 does not resolve extends; --include-merged-configuration errors with 'No image information specified'.
The reference CLI does not resolve extends at read-configuration, so a cyclic extends chain is accepted (echoed literally).
The reference CLI does not resolve extends at read-configuration, so an extends pointing at a nonexistent file is accepted (echoed literally).
The reference CLI recovers from a hard JSONC syntax error by dropping the broken property and accepting the file.
With no devcontainer configuration present at all, the reference CLI rejects rather than inventing an empty config.
The reference CLI accepts and preserves unknown / forward-compatible top-level fields verbatim in read-configuration output.
The reference CLI keeps a bare-string `features` value as raw JSON and accepts it.
The reference CLI keeps a bare-string `forwardPorts` value as raw JSON and accepts it.
